instruction: Implement a rate limiter using the token bucket algorithm.
output:
<think>
Token bucket: tokens accumulate at a fixed rate up to a maximum (bucket size). Each request consumes one token. If the bucket is empty, the request is rejected.

Properties: allows bursts up to bucket size, then limits to the refill rate. This is better than fixed windows because it smooths traffic while allowing short bursts.

instruction: Implement an LRU cache with O(1) operations and explain the hash map + linked list design.
output:
<think>
LRU cache combines hash map (O(1) lookup) with doubly-linked list (O(1) move-to-front). On access, move node to head. On eviction, remove tail. Both O(1) with direct node references from the hash map.

instruction: Explain consistent hashing with virtual nodes for distributed caching.
output:
<think>
Consistent hashing minimizes key remapping when servers change. Regular hash(key) % N remaps almost everything when N changes. Consistent hashing only remaps K/N keys. Virtual nodes (150 per server) ensure even distribution across the ring.

instruction: Implement a URL shortener and explain the math behind short code generation.
output:
<think>
URL shortening maps a long URL to a short code. With base62 (a-z, A-Z, 0-9), a 7-character code supports 62^7 = 3.5 trillion URLs. A counter-based approach guarantees uniqueness. Hash-based approach needs collision handling. In practice, use a global counter encoded in base62.
